title: nvm_list: out_id==0 guard needs infinite-loop explanation
description: nvm_list() lines 106-109: 'if out_id == 0 { break; }' has a comment 'should not occur after a successful response' but does not explain the consequence of removing the guard. Without it, start_id stays 0, the next iteration sends the same query, gets the same out_id == 0 response, and the loop never terminates. A contributor debugging this and seeing 'should not occur' may remove the guard as apparent dead code. The comment should say: 'Guard against an infinite loop: if the server returns 0 after a success response, start_id would not advance and the next request would repeat indefinitely.'
